rationales.self_direction: He says it was "good business practice to go along" with KTV, showing he let external expectations steer him instead of acting on his own priorities at home.
rationales.achievement: He stayed out for an "important account" he had "been courting for months," prioritizing the deal and relationship-building.
rationales.conformity: Going to KTV because it was "good business practice" reflects deliberate compliance with business norms.
rationales.benevolence: When Ma had a dizzy spell, he replied "OK take care of her, back late" and admits he chose "convenient over present."

rationales.achievement: "Tanaka confirmed the contract this morning" shows the work push produced the business result he was aiming for.
rationales.benevolence: He recognizes Lifen "notices when I choose work over home" and that the night added to her tally, pointing to neglected closeness at home.

rationales.self_direction: He told Yuxin she should "talk to her teammates, set expectations" instead of silently carrying everyone, endorsing a more self-authored stance.
rationales.benevolence: He stayed on the phone for "almost forty minutes," listened closely, and says these family moments are what make everything "make sense."

rationales.achievement: He skipped the meeting because "a shipment got held at customs," putting the business problem first.
rationales.benevolence: He knows Haojie "wanted me there specifically" and admits he broke that promise, leaving his son unsupported.
He took Haojie out for "beef noodles after school" to spend time together the day after letting him down.

rationales.tradition: The family rallying around Ma's birthday with her favorite fish, a bakery cake, and a handmade card reflects continuity in familiar family rituals.
rationales.benevolence: He says this is what he has been building: everyone in the family deciding to "show up for each other" without being told.

rationales.tradition: Driving Ma to visit Auntie Fang because that family connection matters reflects honoring an elder-centered obligation rather than dropping it.
rationales.benevolence: He will take Ma because she will "worry about Auntie Fang all week" otherwise, showing care that extends through close family ties.

rationales.self_direction: He defends the pivot as "reading the room" and rejects staying with a product line just because others are comfortable with it.
rationales.stimulation: He refuses to spend "the next ten years" making the same shelving unit and is energized by a new urban farming direction.
rationales.achievement: He is focused on proving the new concept, saying "We just need six months to prove the concept."
rationales.power: He bristles at Pieter questioning him "in front of Anouk and Daan," stressing that it was a call he makes.
rationales.security: He abandons existing retail traction for a riskier pivot, explicitly rejecting the safer established line.
rationales.conformity: He says if he had listened to "the cautious voices" they would still be making products nobody wanted, showing active resistance to pressure to stay conventional.

rationales.self_direction: He says he "built my whole life to avoid ordinary," defining himself around choosing a less conventional path.
rationales.stimulation: Terracotta planters in Lisbon and a life built to avoid "ordinary" show attraction to novelty over routine domestic life.
rationales.hedonism: He ate "a cheese sandwich at my desk" and later microwaved leftovers that "tasted like nothing," treating pleasure and comfort as afterthoughts.
rationales.achievement: The entry is packed with "back-to-back calls" and supplier work, showing sustained effort toward business progress.
rationales.security: He contrasts his current life with the ordinary kitchen-homework-practice rhythm he cannot reach, reflecting a path that sacrifices predictability.
rationales.benevolence: He admits he may "throw money at the gap where my presence should be," naming how he is not showing up for his sons.

rationales.self_direction: A two-year lease feels like "a prison sentence," and he pushes for a shorter term to preserve freedom of movement.
rationales.stimulation: He is absorbed by design problems before dawn and rejects Joost's settled corporate path, preferring a more dynamic life.
rationales.hedonism: He kept working until "it was already dark and I had not eaten," with food reduced to takeout eaten standing up.
rationales.achievement: He sketches before brushing his teeth, emails three suppliers, and keeps going until his neck hurts, showing intense work drive.
rationales.security: Signing anything "for a fixed term makes my chest tight," revealing discomfort with stability and long-term lock-in.

rationales.stimulation: He hears the accusation that he keeps choosing "the next interesting thing over the predictable thing that happens to include you," treating novelty as part of the problem.
rationales.benevolence: He decides "I am going to Utrecht this weekend. No excuses," and hands the Valencia call to Pieter to be there for Matteo.

rationales.stimulation: The breakthrough comes at a "climbing wall" where challenge, focus, and "a bit of nerve" finally draw Matteo in.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the weekend with his sons, keeps trying to reach Matteo, and is moved enough to cry on the drive home.

rationales.self_direction: He closes the Milan competition page because "I know what happens," showing a deliberate attempt to steer himself rather than chase every enticing detour.
rationales.achievement: He says "A whole week gone and I cannot tell you what I accomplished" and admits he does not know what it adds up to.
rationales.security: With the supplier falling through and the business back to "square one," the session is marked by instability rather than solid footing.

rationales.hedonism: He takes time to watch the barges and says "The water helped," treating simple sensory relief as something worth pausing for.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the stable consultancy model and says he would "rather fail at something new every two years than succeed at the same thing for twenty."
rationales.stimulation: He keeps "chasing whatever is around the next corner" and explicitly prefers the new over a settled repeating career.
rationales.power: Pieter's resignation leaves him reacting rather than directing events, and he feels like "the kind of person people eventually need a break from."
rationales.security: The contrast with "better pay, predictable hours" and his own uncertain next quarter shows a life that runs against stability.

rationales.stimulation: She says she wants 'a few more surprise elements' and feels a 'shape missing from my week,' framing her current routine as stagnant rather than enlivening.
rationales.benevolence: She spent time making banana bread with Olive and painting rocks in the backyard, centering the day around caring for and being with her child.

rationales.stimulation: Three hours 'watching kids run in circles' while holding juice boxes is described as passive and dull, not energizing or novel.
rationales.hedonism: She ends the outing by saying 'fine' and going to bed at 8:30, signaling endurance rather than enjoyment.
rationales.benevolence: She accepts the day because 'Kids had fun. That's the point,' clearly prioritizing their enjoyment over her own.

rationales.self_direction: She signed up for a Thursday ceramics class and arranged for Kyle to handle bedtime, making a choice that protects time for herself.
rationales.stimulation: Starting a new ceramics class and looking forward to getting her hands dirty with something unfamiliar shows an active move toward novelty.
rationales.hedonism: She is 'already looking forward' to the class for the sheer tactile pleasure of doing something that isn't childcare drudgery.

rationales.self_direction: She took two uninterrupted hours for ceramics and describes feeling like she'd 'briefly been someone else,' suggesting a deliberate reclaiming of a sidelined part of herself.
rationales.stimulation: The new studio, the messy first attempts, and the thrill of driving home with the windows down show her leaning into novelty and sensory intensity.
rationales.hedonism: She says she had fun and lingers on the loud music, leftover pasta, and vivid physical messiness as a rare, pleasurable high point.

rationales.self_direction: She says 'I could go for a weekend. I won't' and then returns to tomorrow's lunch plans and Target run, underscoring how little freedom she feels to follow her own impulses.
rationales.stimulation: She longs for 'one day where I don't know what's going to happen next' and fantasizes about driving with 'No map,' which highlights how trapped she feels in routine.
rationales.hedonism: The night is framed around insomnia, warm wine, and wanting a day to 'just follow whatever sounds interesting,' suggesting her present life is starving that part of her.

rationales.self_direction: She says her own needs are buried 'somewhere on page twelve of a to-do list,' showing how little room she gives herself to choose for herself.
rationales.hedonism: She names herself as 'bored and restless' and notes that nobody asks what she needs, which frames pleasure as chronically deferred.
rationales.conformity: She keeps reciting that 'every parent feels this way' and that others have 'real problems,' policing her own feelings to stay within the script of the grateful parent.
rationales.benevolence: She describes herself as the person who asks everyone else what they need and keeps four other humans organized, placing close others' needs ahead of her own.

rationales.self_direction: She returns to ceramics and contrasts this with days that 'happened to you,' showing she deliberately chose an activity that feels self-directed.
rationales.stimulation: The class remains a fresh, engaging outlet, and she describes the physical challenge as a 'good sore' rather than routine exhaustion.
rationales.hedonism: She enjoys the class enough to call the soreness 'Good sore,' treating the experience itself as rewarding.
rationales.achievement: Making 'a bowl that actually looks like a bowl' and hearing her centering is improving reflects concrete skill progress and competence.

rationales.self_direction: Her jealousy is about Kyle having something 'just for you' that 'nobody scheduled for you,' which highlights her own lack of personal control over time.
rationales.hedonism: She is not jealous of Tahoe itself but of having something pleasurable to anticipate without first coordinating childcare, suggesting that kind of enjoyment feels unavailable to her.

rationales.self_direction: She says every time she carves out 'something that's mine,' it gets swallowed by family demands, showing how little freedom she feels to protect her own space.
rationales.stimulation: Missing ceramics and watching everyone else's photos turn her one novel outlet back into more caretaking, cutting off the freshness she had been seeking.
rationales.hedonism: She describes missing class as 'something being taken away,' framing the loss as a direct loss of enjoyment.
rationales.benevolence: She stays in Olive's room rubbing her back while she sleeps, putting her daughter's immediate comfort ahead of her own plans.

rationales.self_direction: She says she became someone who 'needs permission to enjoy things' and defaults to 'maybe' instead of choosing what she wants, showing blocked autonomy.
rationales.stimulation: What she misses is 'the feeling' of surprise and walking into a room and not knowing what's about to happen, which casts her current life as overly predictable.
rationales.hedonism: She explicitly says she wants to have her own good times and feels unable to simply enjoy things without permission.
rationales.conformity: Calling 'maybe' a way to say no without defending it shows she routinely softens her real desires to avoid friction.
rationales.benevolence: Her fallback is to 'keep making lunches,' a clear image of continuing to prioritize everyday family care over her own wants.

rationales.conformity: He focuses on getting through the group meeting 'without making a fool of myself,' showing strong attention to fitting in and not drawing negative notice.
rationales.benevolence: After class he drove home to check on Mom, making care for a close family member part of the same day.

rationales.self_direction: He insists the assignment only required accurate summary and resists stating 'what's YOUR position,' showing reluctance to author his own stance.
rationales.conformity: He emphasizes proper citation, correct formatting, and not 'broadcasting' opinions, favoring rule-following and restraint over speaking out.

rationales.self_direction: He had 'thirty years of evidence' but 'just nodded' when the group disagreed, suppressing his own judgment instead of asserting it.
rationales.security: His objection is that eliminating paper backups is 'reckless' because he has seen digital systems crash, so he is clearly prioritizing stability and safeguards.
rationales.conformity: Even though he thought the plan was risky, he went along because 'they all agreed so fast,' choosing harmony over voicing dissent.

rationales.achievement: He missed the reading, plans to skim before class, and admits he is 'running on fumes,' which shows his performance standards slipping under the strain.
rationales.conformity: He says 'okay' to Karen and changes the subject rather than telling her what he really thinks, again choosing smoothness over confrontation.
rationales.benevolence: He spent the weekend rearranging his mother's home after her fall and is carrying the day-to-day responsibility for her care.

rationales.self_direction: He went to office hours, asked directly about the feedback, and begins to see that his own experience can belong in his work.
rationales.achievement: Seeking feedback, rereading the paper, and realizing how he could improve it reflects an active effort to do better and produce stronger work.

rationales.self_direction: He writes the implementation risks section from his own county experience and notes that he finally used knowledge he usually kept to himself.
rationales.achievement: He drafted three pages in two hours, the group praised it as 'really good,' and he clearly values that recognition of competent work.

rationales.self_direction: He says he 'won't' tell Karen what he really thinks and instead absorbs the tension, showing how thoroughly he suppresses his own position.
rationales.conformity: He chooses the version of the conversation 'where at least nobody is yelling,' prioritizing harmony even when it leaves him carrying the burden.
rationales.benevolence: He is the one driving to Duluth, moving furniture, filling prescriptions, and trying to manage what his mother needs.

rationales.self_direction: He remembers always letting Linda choose because picking wrong might disappoint her, showing a pattern of surrendering his own preferences.
rationales.conformity: He says it was 'Easier to let her decide and go along,' which directly reflects deference and avoidance of conflict.

rationales.self_direction: When asked a follow-up, he gave 'an actual answer from what I know' instead of hiding behind summary, showing more ownership of his own voice.
rationales.achievement: He practiced, knew the material cold, got through the presentation, and took pride in having survived something he dreads.

rationales.conformity: He backs off after ninety seconds because he does not want to 'corner' his mother, favoring relational restraint over pressing harder.
rationales.benevolence: He drives to Duluth late at night and frames the whole conversation around helping his mother without making her feel diminished.

rationales.self_direction: He tells Karen she is welcome to come to Duluth and be firm herself, then notices the lighter feeling that came from finally not absorbing everything.
rationales.achievement: The B-plus, the note 'this is your voice,' and his satisfaction with the finished policy brief all point to recognized competence and progress.
rationales.conformity: He says this is the 'First time in a long time' he did not just absorb what Karen threw at him, marking a clear break from his usual restraint.

rationales.self_direction: She enrolled him despite the teacher saying he "wasn't ready" and admits the push is tied to others' children, so neither her judgment nor his own choice is really leading.
rationales.achievement: She treats his Olympiad prep and report cards like a "scoreboard" and pushes him to "rise to the challenge," making performance the central goal.
rationales.power: Arjun "doesn't know I enrolled him," and she expects he will simply "nod" and do the work, showing a top-down decision made for him.
rationales.conformity: Her thinking is driven by what "Kavitha aunty's son" and "Priya" will say at family gatherings, and she relies on Arjun being "obedient" to carry that expectation.
rationales.benevolence: She questions whether this is "for him" or for her own standing, and admits she keeps adding pressure even as he quietly absorbs it.

rationales.security: With Amma "seventy-eight" and unable to stand long, learning the dish "properly this time" is a way to preserve a stable family routine before it is lost.
rationales.tradition: The whole moment centers on pongal rice made the way Amma has done it "for sixty years" and on learning it by standing beside her until "my hands remember."
rationales.benevolence: She is moved that Amma got up at five because Arjun missed the dish, and her response is to learn how to carry that care forward for the family.

rationales.achievement: She spends the morning reorganizing the shelf, driving children to lessons, and managing a week where the "calendar is a Tetris board," showing disciplined execution of responsibilities.
rationales.conformity: Her explanation is simply "Amma asked, I did it," which shows willing compliance with family expectations.
rationales.tradition: Repositioning the Lakshmi lamp in the prayer room ties the action directly to a religious household practice.

rationales.self_direction: She says she thought "I'd be different but I'm running the same script," showing she falls back into an inherited pattern rather than choosing a new approach.
rationales.power: She says "no before I even thought about it," deciding unilaterally whether Divya can go to the party.
rationales.conformity: Divya already "knows the answer before she asks," which reflects enforcement of expected behavior rather than open negotiation.
rationales.tradition: The conflict is over skipping "the temple youth group meeting," and she recognizes she is replaying the same mother-daughter pattern she grew up with.
rationales.benevolence: Divya's quiet retreat makes clear her needs are not being heard, and the narrator admits she is repeating a script that shuts her daughter down.

rationales.achievement: She came with "the spreadsheet ready this time" when her budget was questioned, showing preparation and competence.
rationales.power: She is helping decide the vendor list and stage layout and is ready to defend her budget in the meeting.
rationales.tradition: Organizing the "Pongal committee" directly supports a recurring cultural celebration.

rationales.achievement: Even a "forty-second percentile" result is immediately reframed as a "baseline," keeping the focus on performance improvement.
rationales.security: After being warned he wasn't ready, she still treats the disappointing score as just the cost of pushing harder rather than a sign to choose a safer, steadier path.
Amma's fall and the question of a helper make the entry explicitly about whether the household is still "sustainable" and how to keep everyone safe.
rationales.conformity: She says she "can't be the one" to tell Amma and fears the look that would say she has "betrayed something," showing how strongly social disapproval governs her.
rationales.tradition: What stops her is Amma's established order: the "morning puja," the spice boxes, and the way the house is run.
rationales.benevolence: She spends the weekend supporting Sanjay at every match and is deeply concerned about how to get Amma help without hurting her.

rationales.achievement: She highlights that Divya has been "grinding at it for months" and celebrates that she played the Mendelssohn piece "clean, start to finish."
rationales.benevolence: Her "chest crack open" response and the tender "beautiful, kanna" show close emotional care and pride in her daughter as a person, not just a result.

rationales.achievement: The moment centers on Arjun's score rising to the "sixty-first percentile," and her first response is still "good, keep going," keeping progress front and center.
rationales.benevolence: She notices he looked "happy at the counter" and regrets turning it into another waypoint, wishing she had simply told him "that's really good, Arjun."

rationales.self_direction: She is drawn to the Kenya project because it lets her "build the curriculum from scratch," and says staying makes her feel like she's "turning to stone."
rationales.stimulation: Her "heart raced" at the thought of going somewhere new, while the current school feels suffocatingly repetitive.
rationales.security: She is willing to give up the beautiful farmhouse and stable post because rootedness feels like a trap rather than protection.
rationales.conformity: The ceremony's message about "commitment" and "putting down roots" is exactly what she rejects, even if she knows she's "supposed to feel grateful."
rationales.tradition: She feels guilt for wanting to leave again because it means breaking with the settled life and continuity others around her want.
rationales.benevolence: Elsa asks, "don't you ever want to just... stay?" and the honest answer is still "no," putting her restlessness ahead of her daughter's wish for steadiness.

rationales.self_direction: She actually books the Nairobi flight and frames it as a chance to "explore," choosing movement over staying put.
rationales.stimulation: A "preliminary visit" to Nairobi is pursued for its newness and possibility rather than for any established routine.
rationales.security: She leaves the farmhouse question for "later," showing little interest in locking down the practical stability before she goes.
rationales.conformity: Taking a sabbatical to chase this trip breaks with the settled expectations of her school and home life.

rationales.self_direction: He spends the afternoon softening each comment around parents' likely reactions instead of saying things in the plainest terms.
rationales.conformity: He explicitly wants to make sure "no parent should feel embarrassed or caught off guard," prioritizing smooth relationships and social expectations.
rationales.benevolence: He tailors feedback because "different families need different approaches," showing careful concern for how parents and students receive it.

rationales.self_direction: He privately disagrees with the vice-principal but still says, "I nodded too," suppressing his own judgment.
rationales.stimulation: He sees the proposed shake-up as something that "creates chaos in practice," not as an energizing change.
rationales.security: He explicitly prefers a curriculum that has "worked for fifteen years" because students need "structure and clear expectations."
rationales.conformity: He goes along because "new vice-principals need to feel heard" and because nobody else was speaking up.

rationales.self_direction: He says "I should have said something" but keeps walking, letting fear of the scene override his own judgment.
rationales.power: As a teacher he could have intervened, but he deliberately avoids using that authority in the moment.
rationales.security: He repeatedly frames silence as the "practical choice" because he wants to avoid complaints, influential parents, and vice-principal involvement.
rationales.conformity: He does what the other teachers do and says nothing because speaking up would make everyone feel "judged" and create awkwardness.
rationales.benevolence: Mr. Goh is described as "a good man," yet he leaves him undefended even though he suspects the remark hurt him.
rationales.universalism: He allows a public act of disrespect to pass because challenging it would be inconvenient, sacrificing fairness for self-protection.

rationales.self_direction: He repeats the same answers every week and says nothing when his mother waits for more, staying inside a script he does not author.
rationales.stimulation: He notices he has been having "the same conversation" for thirty years and settles into it because it is "familiar."
rationales.security: He ultimately describes the weekly pattern as "comfortable," showing preference for predictability.
rationales.conformity: The call is governed by indirect expectations on both sides, and he plays his part without disrupting the routine.
rationales.tradition: Calling Mother at 7pm "as usual" and maintaining the long-standing family ritual tie the entry to continuity with established ways.

rationales.hedonism: He spends the evening with "a glass of Burgundy" and chooses that private pleasure over dealing with anyone else.
rationales.power: He is enraged that a commission he thought came to him through his Frankfurt and Munich standing is no longer under his control.
rationales.benevolence: He dismisses everyone he could call as useless or judgmental and concludes, "The wine was better company," choosing ego protection over connection.

rationales.hedonism: The open budget and his thought of extending the "Mallorca stay into April" show how quickly the commission is linked to enjoyment and comfort.
rationales.achievement: He presents past work effectively, reads the couple well, and leaves the meeting feeling "solid about the project."
rationales.power: He studies which spouse really decides and plans a presentation that will steer that influence in his favor.

rationales.hedonism: After the dinner he immediately turns to "a 2015 Barolo I'd been saving," centering personal indulgence.
rationales.benevolence: He reduces Christmas at his sister's to "too many relatives, too much noise" and pulls away rather than engaging with close family.

rationales.power: He treats the commission as something that should have been "mine by right" because of the relationships and obligations he built.
rationales.security: He considers withdrawing entirely out of anger, preferring a risky rupture over managing the uncertainty of the competition.
rationales.conformity: He rejects the juried process as "fairness and transparency" imposed by bureaucrats and resists submitting to that rule-bound procedure.

rationales.self_direction: What he envies in Joachim is the freedom to work with "no deadlines, no clients, no obligations," and he wonders when he stopped "just making things."
rationales.power: He is weary of every conversation being about "who gets credit, who controls the timeline, who has final say," and feels diminished by how much that status game rules him.

rationales.hedonism: He enjoys the evening's luxuries, from the "grey Zegna" and "Patek" to the satisfaction of the event itself.
rationales.achievement: He frames the recognition as something he has "earned... through decades of difficult work," linking the night to demonstrated accomplishment.
rationales.power: What matters most is the "standing" that lets him walk into a room and have people know who he is.

rationales.self_direction: "No audience. Nothing to prove to anyone" captures how strongly he comes alive when the work is self-directed rather than performed for others.
rationales.hedonism: Despite the sore back, he says he felt "better than I have in weeks" and caps the day with a good Riesling and satisfaction in what he made.
rationales.power: He explicitly contrasts the day with the usual "performance" and says there was "nothing to prove to anyone," rejecting the status dynamic that usually drives him.

rationales.self_direction: When asked directly, he tells the Beckers the subtle piece is "better art," choosing his own standard over the bigger, showier option.
rationales.achievement: He brings three serious maquettes, makes a clear artistic judgment, and is pleased they chose the work he believes is better.
rationales.power: He knowingly talks himself out of "substantial money" and says the "status game has diminishing returns," showing reduced commitment to prestige and dominance.
rationales.universalism: He favors the version that "echoes the water table beneath" and "belonged to the land," showing care for fit with the broader environment.

rationales.achievement: The focus on "18 out of 20" and the comparison to someone who "always got full marks" frames the moment around strong academic standards and performance.
rationales.conformity: She "just changed the subject" instead of confronting the comment, actively holding herself back to keep dinner smooth.
rationales.tradition: She accepts that "comparison is how she shows she cares," treating the elder's familiar family style as something to accommodate rather than challenge.
rationales.benevolence: She tells Jia Hui she is proud, puts the test on the fridge, and is pained when she sees her daughter's face fall.

rationales.achievement: Paying for an enrichment center and wanting Jia Hui to come home with "full marks" shows active investment in measurable success.
rationales.security: She keeps "opening the banking app" and worries about whether staying home was "worth it" years from now, showing vigilance about financial and future stability.
rationales.benevolence: She spends "$800 a month" and questions whether she "did right by her," putting her daughter's future ahead of her own career certainty.

rationales.self_direction: Her standard is being set by outside pressure like "other mothers are doing more flashcards" and "every other parent," not by her own independent judgment.
rationales.hedonism: She is "running on four hours of sleep," shaking from coffee, and still pushing through chores and phonics, overriding comfort and rest.
rationales.achievement: She treats being "behind" as personal failure and jumps straight to PSLE and catch-up, making performance and competence central.
rationales.conformity: She measures herself against what "every other parent" is doing and feels pressure to match the tuition-class norm.
rationales.benevolence: She stays up holding Ming through nightmares, protects Wei Hong's sleep before his presentation, and keeps caring for both kids despite exhaustion.

rationales.self_direction: She immediately writes down the teacher's advice, tracks progress obsessively, and hides that they use only one class, showing how strongly outside expectations are steering her choices.
rationales.achievement: Keeping a notebook "for tracking both kids' progress" and ordering "three new comprehension workbooks" shows active pursuit of better results.
rationales.security: She inventories bonuses, CPF, emergency savings, job loss, medical bills, and inflation, then says she would "rather be overprepared than caught off guard."
rationales.conformity: She lies that they are still "exploring options" because she does not want to admit they only do one enrichment class, signaling sensitivity to parent-group judgment.
rationales.tradition: Her mother's saying to "save for nine winters" becomes wisdom she now embraces rather than dismisses as superstition.
rationales.benevolence: She responds quickly to the teacher's feedback, tracks both kids closely, and worries about protecting the family from future financial or medical shocks.

rationales.conformity: She stays inside the family script of comparing "which schools" and "percentile rankings" instead of interrupting it or redirecting the moment.
rationales.benevolence: She realizes Jia Hui was left "in the corner playing with her cousin's old toys" and that she "should have let her be a child," suggesting she failed to prioritize her daughter's immediate needs.

rationales.achievement: She defends the "third workbook" because Jia Hui must "be prepared" and "not struggle later," keeping readiness and performance at the center.
rationales.power: Saying "I'm the one here every day, I see what she needs" asserts decision-making authority over Wei Hong's concern.
rationales.benevolence: After hearing Jia Hui "looked tired," she still centers workbooks, WhatsApp comparisons, and being "ready for the real world" over the child's current wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She deliberately turns away from "metrics, targets, quarterly reviews" and says "she's not a project," reclaiming her own way of parenting.
rationales.hedonism: She says yes to "just play," builds a blanket fort, reads picture books, and treasures that it was "the best day."
rationales.achievement: She questions the whole habit of "keeping score" and using "developmental benchmarks," explicitly rejecting the performance mindset she had imposed at home.
rationales.benevolence: She spends two hours fully present with Jia Hui and realizes her daughter "just wants her mother to be there, actually there."

rationales.self_direction: He has the slide up and the data ready, but goes quiet instead of insisting on his own line once Marcus cuts in.
rationales.power: Marcus talks over him, the room moves on, and he admits that saying nothing "definitely made me look weaker," showing a clear loss of standing.
rationales.conformity: He stays silent because he does not want to be the one "making a scene," prioritizing decorum over pushing back.

rationales.achievement: Even after he has "run the numbers," the team is still "burning hours on workarounds," so competent execution is being actively blocked.
rationales.power: He says "none of that matters because it's Dave's signature on the line," making the core frustration a lack of control over what happens next.

rationales.self_direction: He believed in the vendor swap and had "solid margins data," but he "folded" as soon as the procedural pushback came.
rationales.power: Being shot down "in front of the entire ops meeting" and giving up the proposal leaves him with little influence over the decision.
rationales.conformity: Once Karen says "that's not how we onboard vendors here," he goes along with the established process instead of openly challenging it.

rationales.achievement: He stays focused on the fact that they are "paying twelve percent more than we need to" and plans to reframe the idea to get the result.
rationales.power: Instead of dropping it, he decides to bring it back as "a cost reduction initiative," a strategic move to regain traction and influence the outcome.

rationales.achievement: He highlights being "second highest in the region, up four percent" and wants leadership to see "what my team has built," centering measurable success.
rationales.power: Saying yes immediately to the leadership summit and pointing to having "a team," "numbers," and "a track record" shows comfort with visibility and influence.
Derek explaining the system he designed and then going to Tanya for "how things work around here" makes him feel his role and authority are being bypassed.
rationales.conformity: He "let him finish because I didn't want to embarrass him in front of everyone," restraining himself to preserve group decorum.
